input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project. I can use `dotnet watch test` which will watch for file changes and re-run tests automatically. Let me start it up in async mode so the user can interact with it.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-2c689873e0d849e68e97e5b7d35b0084 && dotnet watch test --no-restore 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "dotnet-watch"}

data.content: There's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so `dotnet watch test` can run properly.
data.reasoningText:
There's a build error - `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Let me also check the watch output. But first, the main issue is the build error. The user asked me to set up hot reload, and the test has a build error plus a logic bug (express returns 10.00 but the test expects 15.00).

Let me fix the build error first, then let the watch do its job. Actually, the user just asked to set up hot reload - let me fix the build error so the watch can run, then start the watch properly.

Can you set it up for me?" - they want the watch set up so they can iterate. Let me fix the build error, then ensure watch is running so they can see the failing test and iterate.
